title: Brevo vs AWeber (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Brevo and AWeber email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which platform suits your needs.
h1: Brevo vs AWeber: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: Brevo and AWeber are both established email marketing platforms with free tiers, but they serve different audiences and use cases. Brevo positions itself as a comprehensive all-in-one CRM suite with multi-channel capabilities, while AWeber focuses on simplicity for bloggers and solopreneurs. Understanding their key differences will help you choose the right tool for your email marketing strategy.
choose_a_if: You need multi-channel marketing (SMS, WhatsApp, live chat) beyond email
You require a robust CRM system integrated with email marketing
You send transactional emails at scale with API requirements
You want to accommodate 100,000+ free contacts without upgrading
choose_b_if: You're a blogger or solopreneurs who values simplicity over features
You want the lowest possible entry price for paid plans ($15/month)
You need RSS-to-email functionality to automate blog promotions
You prefer a straightforward interface without overwhelming options

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Brevo is better for businesses needing multi-channel marketing, CRM capabilities, and transactional emails. AWeber is better for bloggers and solopreneurs prioritizing simplicity and ease of use. Both have strong ratings, but serve different user profiles.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Brevo or AWeber?
faq.a: AWeber has a lower entry price at $15/month for paid plans compared to Brevo's $25/month. However, Brevo's free tier allows 100,000 contacts versus AWeber's 500, making Brevo significantly more cost-effective for growing businesses staying on the free plan longer.

faq.q: Can I switch from AWeber to Brevo?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your contact lists and email history. Most email marketing tools support CSV imports, making migration straightforward. You may want to recreate automation sequences manually, as they typically don't transfer between platforms automatically.
